1/23/21, 4:43 PM - +91 97408 38609: #include <stdio.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <ctype.h>
int main()
{
    char str[1000];

    int i = 0, word = 1, sp = 0, digit = 0, vowel = 0;

    printf("enter a string\n");
    gets(str);

    while (i != '\0')
    {
        if (str[i] == ' ' && str[i + 1] != ' ')
        {
            word++;
        }

        switch (toupper(str[i]))
        {
        case 'A':
        case 'E':
        case 'I':
        case 'O':
        case 'U':
            vowel++;
        }

        if (isdigit(str[i]))
        {
            digit++;
        }

        if (str[i]== ' ')
        {
            sp++;
        }

        i++;
    }
    printf("word %d\n", word);
    printf("digit %d\n", digit);
    printf("vowel  %d\n", vowel);
    printf("space %d\n", sp);

    return 0;
}

1/24/21, 11:37 AM - +91 87770 46270: if not possible then you can use this approach,




If you want a single button that can be checked or unchecked, use a checkbox.

It is possible (but normally not relevant) to uncheck a radio button in JavaScript, simply by setting its checked property to false, e.g.

<input type=radio name=foo id=foo value=var>
<input type=button value="Uncheck" onclick=
"document.getElementById('foo').checked = false">

1/25/21, 10:15 PM - +91 99032 73301: public class OddDivisor {
 
	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
		int t=sc.nextInt();
		while(t-->0) {
			 long n = sc.nextLong();  
			
		
				if (powerOfTwoGeneral(n)) {
					System.out.println("NO");
				}
				else
					System.out.println("YES");
			}
				
			}
 
	 
	public static boolean powerOfTwoGeneral(long n)
	 {
	 while(n%2==0)
	 {
	 n=n/2;
	 }
	 if(n==1)
	 {
	 return true;
	 }
	 else
	 {
	 return false;
	 }
	 }
 
}

1/25/21, 10:18 PM - +91 88253 53912: import java.util.Scanner;
 
public class oddDivisor {
 
    static void recursiveSol(long n){
        if(n%2==1&&n>1) {
            System.out.println("YES");
            return;
        }
        else if(n==2){
            System.out.println("NO");
            return;
        }
        n /= 2;
        if(n%2==0&&n>1) recursiveSol(n);
    }
 
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
        int t = sc.nextInt();
        while(t--!=0){
            long  n = sc.nextInt();
            recursiveSol(n);
        }
    }
}

public class certainSum {
 
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
        int t = sc.nextInt();
        while(t--!=0){
            int n = sc.nextInt();
            if((n>=2020)&&(n%2020==0 || n%2021==0 || n%2020==1 || n%2021==2020)){
                System.out.println("Yes");
            }
            else System.out.println("No");
        }
    }

using namespace std;
int main() {
     int n;

     int arr[n];
     bool flag;
     for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
     {
         cin>>arr[i];
     }
     for (int i = 0; i < n/2; i++)
     {
         if (arr[i]==arr[n-i-1])

             flag =true;
         }
         else flag= false;
         
     }
     if (flag!=false)

     }
     else

     }

}

2/5/21, 7:38 AM - +91 96320 97691: Listen brother, it's not really if and else actually. There are a lot things you should think over. 
First, if the startup is a non-funded startup and is has been in existence for fewer than 5 years, you have no clue when it shuts it's doors and what not. If it's a funded one like Swiggy, Zomato, PharmEasy, Cred, it's absolutely fine. 

Second, your growth would actually depend on how much effort you put in. Be it an MNC and/or a startup, it will only be how much you are willing to put in every day that matters. 

MNCs have a kind of a less intense work rate so you can take your sweet time, settle in cus' the employee base in generally huge and you will integrated into the projects slowly. 

Startups have a very intense work culture because obviously. 

As far as growth is considered, it will totally depend on two things

1. The type of work you perform. If you have joined a startup and have been alloted Testing, then you the growth would be a bit challenging. 

2. The extra tech, tools and technology you take up. Suppose you are alloted testing in one of the companies. But you really want to switch to development. 
You might need to show them the proof of it. So pick up a very popular tech and build 5-8 remarkable projects on it. And that is the only way of your growth. 

Cus' people are fired everyday in both the scale companies due to poor performance. So, make up your mind accordingly.

using namespace std;
int main() {
     int t;
     cin>>t;
     while (t--)
     {
         int sum1=0;
         int sum2=0;
        int sum=0;
         int n;
         cin>>n;
         int arr[n];
         for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
         {
             cin>>arr[i];
         }
         for(int i=0;i<n;i++){
            
             if(i>=0&&i<n-1)
            {
             sum1+=abs(arr[i]-arr[i+1]);
            }
             else if (i==n-1)
            {
               sum2+=arr[n-1]-arr[0];
            
            }
           
         }
             sum=sum1+sum2; 
            cout<<sum<<endl;
     } 
     
     return 0;
}

2/9/21, 12:44 PM - +91 70115 09528: package stringDs;

public class LexicographicalRank {
    static final int x = 256;

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println(lexicographicalRank("string"));
        System.out.println(factorial(5));
    }

    public static int lexicographicalRank(String s) {
        int rank = 0;
        int arr[] = new int[x];
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            arr[s.charAt(i)]++;
        }
        for (int i = 1; i < arr.length; i++) {
            arr[i] += arr[i - 1];
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length() - 1; i++) {
            rank += (arr[s.charAt(i) - 1]) * factorial(s.length() - i - 1);
            for (int j = arr[s.charAt(i)]; j < arr.length; j++) {
                arr[j]--;
            }
        }
        return rank + 1;
    }

    public static int factorial(int n) {
        int fact = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
            fact *= i;
        }
        return fact;
    }
}

2/15/21, 5:06 PM - +91 70015 89718: #include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main()
{
    int n, i, j;
    cin >> n;
    char *p = new char(n);
    for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
        cin >> p[i];
    for (i = 0; i < n; i++)
    {
        for (j = 0; j < n; j++)
            cout << p[i] << ":" << p[j] << "\n";
    }
    return 0;
}

2/21/21, 12:15 AM - +91 89871 07870: hello everyone ,I am trying to solve the problem next palindrome where we have to find the immediate next palindrome of a given no. 2/21/21, 12:15 AM - +91 89871 07870: this is my code:-
2/21/21, 12:15 AM - +91 89871 07870: #include<iostream>
#include<iterator>
#include<vector>
using namespace std;

bool check_nines(vector<long long> num,long long size)
{
    long long count=0;
    for(long long i=0;i<size;i++)
    {
        if(num[i]==9)
            count++;
    }
    if (count==size)
        return true;
    else
        return false;
}

void copy(vector<long long> &v,long long low,long long high)
{
    for(long long i=low;i>=0;i--)
            {
                v[high]=v[i];
                high++;
            }
}

void next_num(vector<long long> &v,long long low,long long high)
{
    if(v[low]>v[high])
    {
        copy(v,low,high);
    }
    else if(v[low]<v[high])
    {
        v[low]+=1;
        copy(v,low,high);
        
    }
    else
    {
        long long temp_low=low;
        long long temp_high=high;
        while(temp_low!=0 && temp_high!=v.size()-1)
        {
            if(v[temp_low]!=v[temp_high])
                break;
            temp_high++;
            temp_low--;
        }
        if(v[temp_low]>v[temp_high])
        {
            copy(v,low,high);
        }
        else
        {
            v[low]+=1;
            copy(v,low,high);
        }
    }
}

void next_num2(vector<long long> &v,long long low,long long high,long long mid)
{
    if(v[low]>v[high])
    {
        copy(v,low,high);
    }
    else if(v[low]<v[high])
    {
        if(v[mid]==9)
        {    
            v[mid]=0;
            v[low]+=1;
            copy(v,low,high);
        }
        else
        {    
            v[mid]+=1;
            copy(v,low,high);
        }
        
    }
    else
    {
        long long temp_low=low;
        long long temp_high=high;
        while(temp_low!=0 && temp_high!=v.size()-1)
        {
             if(v[temp_low]!=v[temp_high])
                break;
            temp_high++;
            temp_low--;
        }
        if(v[temp_low]>v[temp_high])
        {
             copy(v,low,high);
        }
        else
        {
            if(v[mid]==9)
            {    
                v[mid]=0;
                v[low]+=1;
                copy(v,low,high);
            }
            else
            {    
                v[mid]+=1;
                copy(v,low,high);
            }
        }
    }
}

void find_next_palindrome(vector<long long> &v,long long size)
{
    if(check_nines(v,size))
    {
        v[0]=1;
        v.push_back(1);
        for(long long i=1;i<size;i++)
            v[i]=0;
    }
    else if(size%2==0)
    {
        long long mid=size/2;
        long long low=mid-1;
        long long high=mid;
        next_num(v,low,high);
    }
    else if(size%2!=0)
    {
        long long mid=size/2;
        long long low=mid-1;
        long long high=mid+1;
        next_num2(v,low,high,mid);
    }
    cout<<"\n";
}

int main()
{
    long long t;
    cin>>t;
    while(t--)
    {
        long long a,count=0,temp;
        cin>>a;
        temp=a;
        while(temp)
        {
            count++;
            temp/=10;
        }
        vector<long long> v(count);
        while(a)
        {
            v[count-1]=a%10;
            a/=10;
            count--;
        }
        long long size=v.size();
        if(size==1)
            cout<<v[0]+1;
        else
        {
            find_next_palindrome(v,size);
            for(long long i=0;i<v.size();i++)
                cout<<v[i];
        }
        
    }
    return 0;
}

#include<iostream>
#include<cstdio>
#include<string>
using namespace std;

class account
{
	int acc_no;
	string name;
	string type;

	public:
	long int deposit;
	void create_acc()
	{
		cout<<"Enter account number: ";
		cin>>acc_no;
		cout<<"Enter account holder's name: "<<endl;
		cin>>name;
		cin.ignore();
        cout<<"Enter type of account(current/saving): "<<endl;
		cin>>type;
		cout<<"Enter amount to be deposited: ";
		cin>>deposit;
		cout<<"Account created"<<endl;
		}

	void display()
	{
		cout<<"Account no: "<<acc_no<<endl;
		cout<<"Account holder's name: "<<name<<endl;
		cout<<"Account type: "<<type<<endl;
		cout<<"Balance: Rs "<<deposit<<endl;
		
		}
	int acc_number()
	{
		return(acc_no);
		}

	};

void insert()
{
	ofstream f;
	f.open("abc bank.dat", ios::binary|ios::app);
	account a;
	a.create_acc();
	f.write((char*)&a, sizeof(a));
	f.close();
	}

void show()
{
	ifstream f;
	f.open("abc bank.dat", ios::binary|ios::in);
	account a;
	while(f.read((char*)&a, sizeof(a)))
	{	a.display();
	}
	f.close();
	}

void search(int n)
{
	ifstream f("abc bank.dat",ios::binary|ios::in);
	account a;
	while(f.read((char*)&a, sizeof(a)))
	{       int c=a.acc_number();
		if(n==c)
		a.display();
		}
	f.close();
	}

void deposit(int n)
{       long int dep_amt,c;
	account a;
	cout<<"Enter the amount you want to deposit: ";
	cin>>dep_amt;
	ifstream f("abc bank.dat",ios::binary|ios::in);
	ofstream t("temp.dat",ios::binary|ios::out);
	while(f.read((char*)&a, sizeof(a)))
	{       c=a.acc_number();
		if(n==c)
		      a.deposit=a.deposit+dep_amt;
		      t.write((char*)&a, sizeof(a));
		      }
		f.close();
		t.close();
		remove("abc bank.dat");
		rename("temp.dat", "abc bank.dat");
		}

void withdraw(int n)
{
	long int withd_amt,c;
	account a;
	cout<<"Enter the amount you want to withdraw: ";
	cin>>withd_amt;
	ifstream f("abc bank.dat",ios::binary|ios::in);
	ofstream t("temp.dat",ios::binary|ios::out);
	while(f.read((char*)&a, sizeof(a)))
	{       c=a.acc_number();
		if(n==c)
		      a.deposit=a.deposit-withd_amt;
		      t.write((char*)&a, sizeof(a));
		      }
		f.close();
		t.close();
		remove("abc bank.dat");
		rename("temp.dat", "abc bank.dat");
		}

void close(int n)
{
	ofstream o("temp.dat",ios::binary|ios::app);
	ifstream i("abc bank.dat",ios::binary|ios::in);
	account a;

	while(i.read((char*)&a, sizeof(a)))
	{       int c=a.acc_number();
		if(n!=c)
		o.write((char*)&a, sizeof(a));
		}
		o.close();
		i.close();
		remove("abc bank.dat");
		rename("temp.dat", "abc bank,dat");
		cout<<"account closed successfully"<<endl;
		}

void modify(int n)
{
	ofstream o("temp.dat",ios::binary|ios::app);
	ifstream i("abc bank.dat",ios::binary|ios::in);
	account a;

	while(i.read((char*)&a, sizeof(a)))
	{       int c=a.acc_number();
		if(n==c)
		a.create_acc();
		o.write((char*)&a, sizeof(a));
		}
		o.close();
		i.close();
		remove("abc bank.dat");
		rename("temp.dat", "abc bank,dat");
		}

int main()
{
	
	account b;
	int x, n;
	char ch;
	cout<<"<><><><><><><>Welcome to ABC BANK<><><><><><><><><>"<<endl;
	do
	{
	cout<<"--------MENU---------"<<endl;
	cout<<"1. Create new account"<<endl;
	cout<<"2. Deposit amount"<<endl;
	cout<<"3. Withdraw amount"<<endl;
	cout<<"4. Check account balance"<<endl;
	cout<<"5. View all account holder's list"<<endl;
	cout<<"6. Close an account"<<endl;
	cout<<"7. Modify account"<<endl;
	cout<<endl<<"Submit choice: ";
	cin>>x;

	switch(x)
	{
		case 1: insert();
			break;
		case 2: cout<<"Enter account number"<<endl;
			cin>>n;
			deposit(n);
			break;
		case 3: cout<<"Enter account number"<<endl;
			cin>>n;
			withdraw(n);
		case 4: cout<<"Enter account number"<<endl;
			cin>>n;
			search(n);
			break;
		case 5: show();
			break;
		case 6: cout<<"Enter account number"<<endl;
			cin>>n;
			close(n);
			break;
		case 7: cout<<"Enter account number"<<endl;
			cin>>n;
			modify(n);
			break;
		default: cout<<"Invalid choice, Try again"<<endl;
		};
		cout<<endl<<"do you want to go back to menu?"<<endl;
		cout<<"press y for yes and n for no"<<endl;
		cin>>ch;
		}while(ch=='y');
		
		}

"""
Created on Sun Feb 21 15:21:13 2021

@author: Abhishek
"""

import os
import xlrd

# import openpyxl


book = xlrd.open_workbook(os.path.join("Max_covering_data.xlsx"))

sh = book.sheet_by_name("facilities")
demand_node = []
facility_site = []
Demand = {}

i = 1
while True:
    try:
        sp = sh.cell_value(i, 0)
        demand_node.append(sp)
        facility_site.append(sp)
        Demand[sp] = sh.cell_value(i, 1)
        i = i + 1

    except IndexError:
        break

sh = book.sheet_by_name("distance")
distance = {}

i = 1
while True:
    try:
        for j in range(1, len(facility_site) + 1):
            distance[i, j] = sh.cell_value(i, j)
        i = i + 1

    except IndexError:
        break

A_ij = {}
D = 30  # limiting distance
Dem_Cov = {}



nodeDict={}

total_demand_covered=0




for i in demand_node:
    for j in facility_site:
        if distance[i, j] <= D:
            A_ij[i, j] = 1
            Dem_Cov = (i, A_ij[i, j] * Demand[j])

            k = sorted(Dem_Cov)

            if k[0] in nodeDict:

                nodeDict[k[0]]+=k[1]

            else:
                nodeDict[k[0]]=k[1]




        else:
            A_ij[i, j] = 0


#print(nodeDict)

dict1=nodeDict

sorted_values = sorted(dict1.values()) # Sort the values
sorted_dict = {}

for i in sorted_values:
    for k in dict1.keys():
        if dict1[k] == i:
            sorted_dict[k] = dict1[k]
            break

#rint(sorted_dict)

#print(list(sorted_dict.keys())[-1])


Final_facilities=[]

Final_facilities.append(  list(sorted_dict.keys())[-1]      )


lastfacility=list(sorted_dict.keys())[-1]


total_demand_covered+=list(sorted_dict.values())[-1]


No_of_facility= len(list(sorted_dict.keys()))

selected_facility_count=1



P = 3  # no of facilities to be located



for i in range(0,P):

    if(bool(sorted_dict)):


        if lastfacility in sorted_dict:

                print(lastfacility)
                print(total_demand_covered)

                del sorted_dict[lastfacility]

                Final_facilities.append(list(sorted_dict.keys())[-1])

                total_demand_covered += list(sorted_dict.values())[-1]

                lastfacility=list(sorted_dict.keys())[-1]

3/9/21, 7:11 PM - +91 78952 57049: #include<bits/stdc++.h>
#define ll long long
using namespace std;
int main(){
    ll t,c,a,b,d,num;
    cin>>t;
    while(t--){
        cin>>c;
        if(c<=3)
          cout<<"0"<<endl;
        else{
            d=0;
            b=0;
            num=2;
            while(!(num>c)){
                num*=2;
                d++;
            }
            d=d-1;
            while(d>=0){
                b|=1ULL<<d;
                d--;
            }
            a=c^b;
            cout<<a*b<<endl;
        }
          
    }
    return 0;
}

class Solution {
    List<List<Integer>> ans=new ArrayList<>();
    List<String> revList=new ArrayList<>();
    Node root=new Node();
    HashSet<Integer> removedInd=new HashSet<>();
    public List<List<Integer>> palindromePairs(String[] words) {
        for(int i=0;i<words.length;i++)
        {
            if(words[i].length()==0)
            {
                gotEmpty(words,i);
                removedInd.add(i);
            }
        }
        for(int i=0;i<words.length;i++)
        {
            if(removedInd.contains(i))
                continue;
            insert(words[i],i);
            StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder(words[i]);
            sb.reverse();
            revList.add(sb.toString());
        } 
       //System.out.println(revList);
        for(int i=0;i<revList.size();i++)
        {
           int ind=search(revList.get(i),i);
            if(ind!=-1)
            {
                List<Integer> temp=new ArrayList<>();
                temp.add(ind);
                temp.add(i);
                ans.add(temp);
            }
        }
        return ans;
    }
    void gotEmpty(String words[],int ind)
    {
        for(int i=0;i<words.length;i++)
        {
            if(i!=ind && isPalindrome(words[i]))
            {
                List<Integer> temp1=new ArrayList<>();
                List<Integer> temp2=new ArrayList<>();
                temp1.add(i);temp1.add(ind);
                temp2.add(ind);temp2.add(i);
                ans.add(temp1);
                ans.add(temp2);
            }
            
        }
    }
    boolean isPalindrome(String str)
    {
        int i=0;
        int j=str.length()-1;
        while(i<j)
        {
            if(str.charAt(i)!=str.charAt(j))
                return false;
            i++;
            j--;
        }
        return true;
    }
    int search(String str,int prefix)
    {
        Node curr=root;
        for(int i=0;i<str.length();i++)
        {
              if(curr.isEnd && curr.index!=prefix)
                return curr.index;
            int ind=str.charAt(i)-'a';
            if(curr.children[ind]==null)
                return -1;
            curr=curr.children[ind];
        }
        if(curr.isEnd  && prefix!=curr.index)
            return curr.index;
        return -1;
    }
    void insert(String str,int index)
    {
        Node curr=root;
        for(int i=0;i<str.length();i++)
        {
            int ind=str.charAt(i)-'a';
            if(curr.children[ind]==null)
                curr.children[ind]=new Node();
            curr=curr.children[ind];
        }
        curr.isEnd=true;
        curr.index=index;
    }
}
class Node
{
    Node children[];
    boolean isEnd;
    int index;
    Node()
    {
        isEnd=false;
        children=new Node[26];
        index=-1;
    }
}

3/24/21, 7:37 PM - +91 70115 09528: package graphDs;

import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.Comparator;
import java.util.LinkedList;
import java.util.PriorityQueue;

public class DijkshtraList {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int vertices = 6;
        Graph graph = new Graph(vertices);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 0, 1, 4);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 0, 2, 3);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 1, 2, 1);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 1, 3, 2);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 2, 3, 4);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 3, 4, 2);
        graph.addEdge(graph, 4, 5, 6);
        dijkshtraList(graph, 0);
    }

    public static void dijkshtraList(Graph graph, int s) {
        PriorityQueue<Pair> pairs = new PriorityQueue<>(new Comparator<Pair>() {
            @Override
            public int compare(Pair o2, Pair o1) {
                return o2.getVertex() - o1.getVertex();
            }
        });
        boolean[] isVisited = new boolean[graph.adjacencyList.length];
        int[] distances = new int[graph.adjacencyList.length];
        Arrays.fill(distances, Integer.MAX_VALUE);
        distances[0] = 0;
        pairs.offer(new Pair(0, distances[0]));
        while (!pairs.isEmpty()) {
            Pair temp = pairs.poll();
            int extractedVertex = temp.getVertex();
            if (!isVisited[extractedVertex]) {
                isVisited[extractedVertex] = true;
                LinkedList<Edge> list = graph.adjacencyList[extractedVertex];
                for (int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++) {
                    Edge edge = list.get(i);
                    int destination = edge.v;
                    if (!isVisited[destination]) {
                        int currentD = distances[destination];
                        int newD = distances[extractedVertex] + edge.weight;
                        if (currentD > newD) {
                            pairs.offer(new Pair(newD, destination));
                            distances[destination] = newD;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        print(distances);
    }

    public static void print(int[] arr) {
        for (int x : arr)
            System.out.print(x + " ");
    }
}

class Edge {
    int u;
    int v;
    int weight;

    Edge(int u, int v, int weight) {
        this.u = u;
        this.v = v;
        this.weight = weight;
    }
}

class Graph {
    LinkedList<Edge>[] adjacencyList;
    int vertices;

    Graph(int vertices) {
        this.vertices = vertices;
        adjacencyList = new LinkedList[vertices];
        for (int i = 0; i < vertices; i++)
            adjacencyList[i] = new LinkedList<>();
    }

    void addEdge(Graph graph, int u, int v, int weight) {
        Edge edge = new Edge(u, v, weight);
        graph.adjacencyList[u].add(edge);
        edge = new Edge(v, u, weight);
        graph.adjacencyList[v].add(edge);
    }
}

class Pair {
    private Integer vertex;
    private Integer distance;

    Pair(Integer vertex, Integer distance) {
        this.vertex = vertex;
        this.distance = distance;
    }

    int getVertex() {
        return this.vertex = vertex;
    }
}

3/31/21, 11:41 PM - +91 70115 09528: package com.company;

import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.Comparator;

public class MinSwaps {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println(minSwaps(new int[]{7, 1, 3, 2, 4, 5, 6}));
    }

    public static int minSwaps(int[] arr) {
        ValueIndexPair[] pairs = new ValueIndexPair[arr.length];
        for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
            pairs[i] = new ValueIndexPair(arr[i], i);
        }
        Arrays.sort(pairs, new Comparator<ValueIndexPair>() {
            @Override
            public int compare(ValueIndexPair o1, ValueIndexPair o2) {
                return o1.value - o2.value;
            }
        });
//        for (int i=0;i<pairs.length;i++){
//            System.out.println(pairs[i].value);
//        }
        int swaps = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
            if (pairs[i].index != i) {
                ValueIndexPair temp = pairs[i];
                pairs[i] = pairs[pairs[i].index];
                pairs[pairs[i].index] = temp;
                swaps++;
                i--;
            }
        }
        return swaps;
    }
}

class ValueIndexPair {
    int value;
    int index;

    ValueIndexPair(int value, int index) {
        this.index = index;
        this.value = value;
    }
}

4/3/21, 9:48 PM - +91 70115 09528: https://www.hackerrank.com/challenges/frequency-queries/problem?h_l=interview&playlist_slugs%5B%5D=interview-preparation-kit&playlist_slugs%5B%5D=dictionaries-hashmaps
4/3/21, 9:48 PM - +91 70115 09528: can anyone help???
4/3/21, 9:48 PM - +91 70115 09528: static List<Integer> freqQuery(List<List<Integer>> queries) {
        List<Integer> list = new ArrayList<>();
        HashMap<Integer, Integer> hashMap = new HashMap<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < queries.size(); i++) {
            int opt=queries.get(i).get(0);
            int act=queries.get(i).get(1);
            if (opt == 1) {
                hashMap.put(act, hashMap.getOrDefault(act, 0) + 1);
            } else if (opt == 2) {
                if (hashMap.containsKey(act)) {
                    if (act == 1) {
                        hashMap.remove(act);
                    } else {
                        hashMap.replace(act,hashMap.get(act)-1);
                    }
                }
            } else if (opt == 3) {
                if (hashMap.containsValue(act)) {
                    list.add(1);
                } else {
                    list.add(0);
                }
            }
            else {
                list.add(0);
            }
        }
        return list;
    }

4/5/21, 8:39 PM - +91 70115 09528: static String isValid(String s) {
        int[] arr = new int[26];
        int c2 = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            arr[s.charAt(i) - 'a']++;
        }
        int c1 = arr[0];
        int a = 1;
        int b = 0;
        int j = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i < arr.length; i++) {
            if (arr[i] == 0) {
                continue;
            }
            if (arr[i] != c1) {
                c2 = arr[i];
                j = i;
                b++;
                break;
            }
            a++;
        }
        if (c2 == 0) {
            return "YES";
        }
        for (int i = j + 1; i < arr.length; i++) {
            if (arr[i] == 0) {
                continue;
            }
            if (arr[i] == c1) {
                a++;
            } else if (arr[i] == c2) {
                b++;
            } else {
                return "NO";
            }
        }
        if ((a == 1 || b == 1) && Math.abs(c1 - c2) <= 1) {
            return "YES";
        } else {
            return "NO";
        }
    }

4/11/21, 7:31 PM - +91 89292 04029: 3
4/11/21, 7:32 PM - +91 80103 79281: How come?
4/11/21, 7:32 PM - +91 89292 04029: 3Rd time, i m asked answer of this q 😅
4/11/21, 7:32 PM - +91 89292 04029: Outer loop N
4/11/21, 7:32 PM - +91 89292 04029: Middle loop N/2
4/11/21, 7:33 PM - +91 89292 04029: Innermost loop, logN
4/11/21, 7:33 PM - +91 89292 04029: As everytime it gets doubled
4/11/21, 7:33 PM - +91 89292 04029: And in computer, base of log is 2
4/11/21, 7:33 PM - +91 89292 04029: So logN
4/11/21, 7:34 PM - +91 89292 04029: And as this loops are inside each other
4/11/21, 7:34 PM - +91 89292 04029: We multiply the complexities of all
4/11/21, 7:34 PM - +91 89292 04029: Which gives
N*(N/2)*logN

4/14/21, 9:44 AM - +91 79035 53605: #include <bits/stdc++.h>
#define IOS ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false); cin.tie(NULL);
#define ll long long int
using namespace std;
int setbits(int n){
    int res=0;
    while (n>0)
    {
       n=(n&(n-1));
       res++;
    }
    return res;
}
int main() {
     int n,m,k,arr[m+1],count=0;
     cin>>n>>m>>k;
     for (int i = 0; i <m+1; i++)
     {
        cin>>arr[i];
     }
     for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
     {
        if (setbits(arr[m])+setbits(arr[i])<=k)
        {
            count++;
        }
        
     }
     cout<<count<<endl;
     return 0;
}

              else
                  lol++;

          }

                 


          if(sarthak>lol)
          {
              cout<<"SARTHAK";
          }
          else if(lol>sarthak)
          {
              cout<<"ANURADHA";
          }
          else if(sarthak==lol){
              cout<<"DRAW";
          }
      }
      return 0;
}

{
    int lol;
    if(k==1)
    {
    return 1;
    }
    else{

    
    lol=k*fact(k-1);
    }

import util.*;
public class solution{
    public static void main(String args[]){
        
    
    Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
    int t = sc.nextInt();
    while(t>0){
        int n= sc.nextInt();
        int [] arr = new int[n];
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++){
            arr [i] = sc.nextInt();
        }
        for(int i=0;i<n-2;i++){
            if (arr[i]*3 == arr[i] +arr[i+1]+arr[i+3])
                continue;
            else
                if (arr[i]==arr[i+1] && arr[i]!=arr[i+2])
                    System.out.print(i+2);
                else if (arr[i]==arr[i+2]&&arr[i]!=arr[i+1])
                    System.out.print(i+1);
                else 
                    System.out.print(i);
        }
        t--;
    }}
}

5/5/21, 12:03 PM - +91 79035 53605: Int sdiff(int arr[], int n)
{
    if (n == 1)
        return -1;
    if (n == 2)
        return 0;
 
    if (arr[0] == arr[1] && arr[0] != arr[2])
        return 3;
    if (arr[0] == arr[2] && arr[0] != arr[1])
        return 2;
    if (arr[1] == arr[2] && arr[0] != arr[1])
        return 1;
 
    for (int i = 3; i < n; i++)
        if (arr[i] != arr[i - 1])
            return i+1;
  
    return -1;
}
int main() {
     int t;
     cin>>t;
     while (t--)
     {
         int n;
         cin>>n;
         int arr[n];
         for (int i = 0; i <n; i++)
         {
             cin>>arr[i];
         }
         cout<<sdiff(arr,n)<<endl;
     }
     
     return 0;
}

5/11/21, 10:45 AM - +91 70930 58788: https://leetcode.com/problems/snakes-and-ladders/
5/11/21, 10:46 AM - +91 70930 58788: For this questions, I have done this soution, but somethings wrong in this solution, anyone debug it??
--------------------------
class Solution {
    HashMap<Integer,Integer> map=new HashMap<>();
    HashSet<Integer> set=new HashSet<>();
    int dest;
    public int snakesAndLadders(int[][] board) {
        int num=1;
        boolean flag=true;
        dest=board.length*board.length;
        for(int i=board.length-1;i>=0;i--)
        {
            if(flag)
            {
                for(int j=0;j<board.length;j++)
                {
                    if(board[i][j]!=-1)
                        map.put(num,board[i][j]);
                    num++;
                }
            }
            else
            {
                for(int j=board.length-1;i>=0;i--)
                {
                    if(board[i][j]!=-1)
                        map.put(num,board[i][j]);
                    num++;
                }
            }
            flag=!flag;
        }
        return bfs();
    }
    int bfs()
    {
        Queue<Node> q=new LinkedList<>();
        q.add(new Node(1,0));
        set.add(1);
        while(q.size()>0)
        {
            Node curr=q.poll();
            if(curr.box==dest)
                return curr.count;
            for(int i=curr.box+1;i<=curr.box+6 && i<=dest;i++)
            {
                if(!set.contains(i))
                {
                    set.add(i);
                    if(map.containsKey(i))
                    {
                        q.add(new Node(map.get(i),curr.count));
                    }
                    else
                        q.add(new Node(i,curr.count+1));
                }
            }
        }
         return -1;
    }
}
class Node
{
    int box, count;
    Node(int box,int count)
    {
        this.box=box;
        this.count=count;
    }
}

5/19/21, 11:43 PM - +91 70115 09528: public static List<Integer> circularPalindromes(String s) {
        // Write your code here
        List<Integer> res = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(s.substring(i));
            sb.append(s.substring(0, i));
            res.add(maxPalindrome(modify(sb.toString())));
        }
        return res;
    }

    public static int maxPalindrome(String s) {
        int c = 0;
        int r = 0;
        int[] lps = new int[s.length()];
        for (int i = 2; i < s.length() - 1; i++) {
            int left = 2 * c - i;
            if (i < r) {
                lps[i] = Math.min(lps[left], r - i);
            }
            while (s.charAt(i + lps[i] + 1) == s.charAt(i - lps[i] - 1)) {
                lps[i]++;
            }
            if (i + lps[i] > r) {
                c = i;
                r = i + lps[i];
            }
        }
        int res = lps[0];
        for (int i = 1; i < lps.length; i++) {
            res = Math.max(res, lps[i]);
        }
        return res;
    }

    public static String modify(String s) {
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        sb.append('@');
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            sb.append('#');
            sb.append(s.charAt(i));
        }
        sb.append('#');
        sb.append('$');
        return sb.toString();
    }

5/27/21, 12:23 PM - +91 91371 77349: ## Read input as specified in the question.
## Print output as specified in the question.
t=int(input())
for j in range(t):
    P,Q=input().split()
    P=int(P)
    Q=int(Q)
if Q==1:
    result=0
    for i in range(1,P+1):
        result=result+i
    print(result) 
elif Q==2:
    result=1
    for i in range(1,P+1):
        result=result*i
    print(result)
else:
    print("-1")

6/2/21, 9:37 AM - +91 70930 58788: import java.util.*;
class Solution {
     List<List<Integer>> adj=new ArrayList<>();
     int res[];
     void solve(){
          Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in);
          int n=sc.nextInt();
          res=new int[n+1];
          for(int i=0;i<=n;i++)
               adj.add(new ArrayList<>());
          for(int i=2;i<=n;i++)
          {
               int val=sc.nextInt();
               adj.get(val).add(i);
          }
          dfs(1);
         for(int i=1;i<=n;i++)
          System.out.print(res[i]+" ");    
     }
     int dfs(int u)
     {
          int tot=0;
          for(int v:adj.get(u)){
               tot+=dfs(v);
          }
          res[u]=tot;
          return tot+1;
     }
     public static void main(String[] args) {
          Solution s=new Solution();
           s.solve();
     }
}

6/5/21, 5:19 PM - +91 91371 77349: import java.util.Scanner;
public class Solution
{
	public static void main(String[] args) 
	{
		/* 
		  Your class should be named Solution.
	 	  Read input as specified in the question.
	 	  Print output as specified in the question.
		*/
    Scanner s=new Scanner (System.in);
	int basicSalary=s.nextInt();
	char grade=s.next().charAt(0);
	double hra=(0.2 * basicSalary);
	double da=(0.5 * basicSalary);
	double pf=0.11 * basicSalary;
	double allowance=0;
	double totalSalary=0;
	if(grade =='A') {
		allowance=1700;
		totalSalary=basicSalary+ hra + da + allowance- pf;
		System.out.println((int)totalSalary);
	}
	else if (grade=='B') {
		allowance=1500;
		totalSalary=basicSalary+ hra + da + allowance- pf;
		System.out.println((int)totalSalary);
	}
	else if (grade=='C'|| ((grade>'C')&&(grade<='Z'))) {
		allowance=1300;
		totalSalary=basicSalary+ hra + da + allowance- pf;
		System.out.println((int)totalSalary);		
	}

	}
}

6/9/21, 5:29 PM - +91 70930 58788: https://leetcode.com/problems/largest-color-value-in-a-directed-graph/
6/9/21, 5:30 PM - +91 70930 58788: for this questions I have done this solution but getting Null pointer exception at line no 38, can anyone find the reason??
6/9/21, 5:30 PM - +91 70930 58788: class Solution {
    int n;
    List<List<Integer>> adj=new ArrayList<>();
    int ind[];
    public int largestPathValue(String colors, int[][] edges) {
        n=colors.length();
        ind=new int[n];
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
            adj.add(new ArrayList<>());
        for(int e[]:edges)
        {
            adj.get(e[0]).add(e[1]);
            ind[e[1]]++;
        }
        Queue<Node> q=new LinkedList<>();
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
        {
            if(ind[i]==0)
            {
                HashMap<Character,Integer> map=new HashMap<>();
                map.put(colors.charAt(i),1);
                q.add(new Node(i,map,1));
            }
        }
        int count=0;
        int ans=0;
        while(q.size()>0)
        {
            Node curr=q.poll();
            int u=curr.val;
            ans=curr.max;
            for(int v:adj.get(u)){
                if(--ind[v]==0)
                {
                    HashMap<Character,Integer> newMap=new HashMap<>();
                    newMap.putAll(curr.map); 
                    newMap.put(colors.charAt(v),newMap.getOrDefault(colors.charAt(v),0)+1);
                    q.add(new Node(v,newMap,Math.max(curr.max,newMap.get(v))));
                }
            }
            count++;
        }
        if(count!=n)
                      return -1;
        return ans;
    }
}
class Node
{
    int val;
    HashMap<Character,Integer> map=new HashMap<>();
    int max;
    Node(){
        
    }
    Node(int val,HashMap<Character,Integer> map,int max)
    {
        this.val=val;
        this.map=map;
        this.max=max;
    }
}

6/11/21, 1:36 PM - +91 70930 58788: <Media omitted>
6/11/21, 1:38 PM - +91 70930 58788: class Solution {
    int dx[]={0,0,1,-1};
    int dy[]={1,-1,0,0};
    int max=0;
    int count=0;
    boolean vis[][];
    public int largestIsland(int[][] grid) {
    vis=new boolean[grid.length][grid[0].length];
      for(int i=0;i<grid.length;i++)
      {
          for(int j=0;j<grid[i].length;j++)
          {
              if(!vis[i][j] && grid[i][j]==1)
              {
                  count=0;
                  dfs(i,j,grid,0);
                  max=Math.max(max,count);
              }
          }
      }
    return max;
    }
    void dfs(int x,int y,int grid[][],int zCount)
    {
        count++;
        vis[x][y]=true;
        for(int i=0;i<4;i++)
        {
            int u=x+dx[i];
            int v=y+dy[i];
            if(valid(u,v,grid))
            {
                if(grid[u][v]==0 && zCount>0)
                    continue;
                if(grid[u][v]==0 && zCount==0)
                    dfs(u,v,grid,zCount+1);
                if(grid[u][v]==1)
                    dfs(u,v,grid,zCount);
                if(grid[u][v]==0)
                    vis[u][v]=false;
            }
        }
    }
    boolean valid(int i,int j,int grid[][])
    {
        return i>=0 && j>=0 && i<grid.length && j<grid[0].length && !vis[i][j];
    }
}

6/12/21, 10:25 AM - +91 78609 74856: void solve()
{
   ll n,m;
   cin>>n>>m;
   ll a[n];
   ff1(i,0,n)
   cin>>a[i];
   ll b[m];
   ff1(i,0,m)
   { 
    cin>>b[i];
   }
   ll mx=-1,an=0;
   for(int i=0,j=0;i<n,j<m;i++,j++)
   {
      if(a[i]==b[j]&&a[i]>mx)
      {
        mx=a[i];
        an++;
      }
   }
   cout<<an;
}

#include<stdlib.h>
struct student 
{
int s_no,age;
float marks;
char name[30],roll_no[10];
}s[20];
int main()
{
int n, i,x;
scanf("%d",&n);
FILE *fr;
fr=fopen("Student_details.txt","rb");
if(fr==NULL) 
{
printf("\nfile cannot be open");
exit(0);
}
switch(n)
{
case 1:
printf("\nenter s_no of student = ");
scanf("%d",&x);
i=0;
while(fread(&s[i],sizeof(struct student),1,fr))
{
if(s[i].s_no==x)
{
printf("%s\n" ,s[i].name);
printf("%d\n" , age);
printf("%c\n" ,roll_no);
printf("%d\n" , s_no.);
printf("%f\n" , marks);
}
}
break;
} 
case 2:
int no.;
printf("enter nuber of student to add in list = ");
scanf("%d",&no.);
for(j=21;j<=20+no.;j++)
{
printf("enter the name of student =" );
scanf("%s", s[j].name);
printf("enter his age = ");
scanf("%d" , &s[j].age);
printf("enter his roll_no = ");
scanf("%c" ,roll_no);
printf("enter his s_no = ");
scanf("%d" , s_no);
printf("enter his marks = ");
scanf("%f" , marks);
fwrite(&s[i], sizeof(struct student),1,fr);
}
break;
case 3:
int mark;
scanf("%d", &mark);
i=0;
while(fread(&s[i],sizeof(struct student) ,1,fr)
{
if(s[i].marks>mark)
{
printf("%d\n" ,s[i].marks);
}
i++;
}
break;
} 
return 0;
}

{

    int q ;

    {

        {

        }
    }

    sum1 = 0;

    {

        sum1 = sum1 + arr[q][j];
        q--;
    }
    cout << sum << sum1 ;
    return 0;
}

public class Solution {

	public static int findSum(ArrayList<Integer> arr) {

		// Write your code here
        int sum=0;
        for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++){
            if((arr[i]%2==0)|(arr[i]%3==0)){
                sum+=arr[i];
            }
        }
		return sum;

	}
}

7/3/21, 12:10 PM - +91 91371 77349: import java.util.ArrayList;

public class Solution {

	public static int findSum(ArrayList<Integer> arr) {

		// Write your code here
        int sum=0;
        for(int i=0;i<arr.size();i++){
            if((arr.get(i)%2==0)|(arr.get(i)%3==0)){
                sum+=arr.get(i);
            }
        }
		return sum;

	}
}

  public:
    int sumOfMatrix(int N, int M, vector<vector<int>> Grid) {
        int sum=0;
        for(int i=0;i<N;i++)
        {
            for(int j=0;j<M;j++)
            {
                sum+=Grid[i][j];
                
            }
        }
        return sum;
        // code here
    }
};

7/13/21, 3:21 PM - +91 91371 77349: class Solution{
    public static int kthSmallest(int[] arr, int l, int r, int k) 
    { 
        
        for(int i=o;i<arr.length-1-1;i++){
            for(int j=0;j<arr.length-i-1;j++){
                if(arr[j]>arr[j+1]){
                    int temp=arr[j];
                    arr[j]=arr[j+1];
                    arr[j+1]=temp;
                }
            }
        }
        return arr[k+1];
    } 
}

7/13/21, 4:39 PM - Anand: You are in an infinite 2D grid where you can move in any of the 8 directions:

 (x,y) to
    (x+1, y),
    (x - 1, y),
    (x, y+1),
    (x, y-1),
    (x-1, y-1),
    (x+1,y+1),
    (x-1,y+1),
    (x+1,y-1)
You are given a sequence of points and the order in which you need to cover the points. Give the minimum number of steps in which you can achieve it. You start from the first point.

Example:

Input: [(0, 0), (1, 1), (1, 2)]
Output: 2
It takes 1 step to move from (0, 0) to (1, 1). It takes one more step to move from (1, 1) to (1, 2).

7/14/21, 7:54 PM - +91 95921 98774: # include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void prime (int a , int b )
{
  for ( int i = a ; i <=b ; i++)
  {
    for (int j = 2 ; j <= a ; j ++)
    {
      bool flag = 0;
      if (i%j == 0)
      {
        flag = 1;
        break;
      }
      if (flag == 0)
      {
        cout << i << " is a prime "<< endl;
      }
      if (i == 2 )
      {
        cout <<"2 is a prime" << endl;
      }
    }
  }
}

int main ()
{
    int a , b ;
    cin >> a >> b ;

    prime(a,b);
    return 0;
}

7/20/21, 7:09 PM - +91 62050 65572: int diagonalSum(int **v,  int N){
    int out=0;
    for(int i=0;i<N;i++)
    {
        for(int j=0;j<N;j++)
        {
            if(i==j || (i==(N-j-1)))
            {
                out = out + v[i][j];
            }
        }
    }
    return out;  
}

cin>>n;
ll k=1,l=n-1,p=n*(n+1);
loop(i,0,n){
  loop(j,0,i)cout<<"  ";
  loop(j,0,n-i){cout<<k<<" ";k++;}
   
  p-=(n-i);

  ll o=p+1;
  loop(u,0,n-i){cout<<o<<" ";o++;}
  cout<<'\n';
}

8/30/21, 12:02 AM - +91 91238 47093: #include<iostream>
#include<vector>
#include<climits>
#include<cmath>
#include<map>
#include<algorithm>
#include<unordered_set>
#include<unordered_map>
using namespace std;
typedef long long ll;
typedef pair<int, int> pii;
typedef pair<ll, ll> pl;
typedef vector<int> vi;
typedef vector<ll> vll;
typedef vector<pii> vpii;
typedef vector<pl> vpl;
typedef vector<vi> vvi;
typedef vector<vll> vvll;
typedef vector<string> vs;
typedef vector<vector<ll>> vvll;
typedef map<ll,bool> mllb;
typedef unordered_set<char> ucst;
typedef unordered_map<ll,bool> umllb;
#define PI 3.1415926535897932384626
#define pb push_back
#define mp make_pair
const ll mod=10e9+7;

class Triplet
{
    public:
        ll gcd;
        ll x;
        ll y;
    Triplet(ll gcd,ll x,ll y)
    {
        this->gcd=gcd;
        this->x=x;
        this->y=y;
    }
};

class Sieve
{
    private:
        void genPrime()
        {
            for(ll i=0;i<n+1;i++)
                Prime.push_back(true);
            Prime.at(0)=false;
            Prime.at(1)=false;
            for(ll i=2;i*i<=n;i++)
                if(Prime[i])
                    for(ll j=i*i;j<=n;j+=i)
                        Prime[j]=false;
        }

    public:
        ll n;
        vector<bool> Prime;

        void printPrime()
        {
            for(ll i=0;i<=n;i++)
                if(Prime[i])
                    cout<<i<<" ";
            cout<<endl;
        }

        ll countPrime(ll l,ll h)
        {
            ll count=0;
            for(ll i=l;i<=h;i++)
                if(Prime[i])
                    count++;
            return count;
        }
    
        bool isPrime(ll x)
        {
            if(x>=0 && x<=n)
            {
                if(Prime.at(x))
                    return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        Sieve(ll n)
        {
            this->n=n;
            genPrime();
        }
};

ll gcd(ll a,ll b) {return a==0?b:gcd(b % a, a);}

Triplet gcdExt(ll a,ll b)
{
    if(b==0)
        return Triplet(a,1,0);
    Triplet smallRes=gcdExt(b,a%b);
    return Triplet(smallRes.gcd,smallRes.y,smallRes.x-(a/b)*smallRes.y);
}

ll multiModInvrs(ll a,ll m)
{
    //if there exist any b (1<=b<=m-1) for which (a.b)modm=1 then b is multiplicative modulo inverse of a and m
    //Multiplicative modulo inverse exists only if gcd(a,m)=1;
    //If returns -1 mean multiplicative modulo inverse doesn't exist.
    Triplet ans=gcdExt(a,m);
    if(ans.gcd==1)
    {
        ll B=ans.x;
        if(B<0)
            B+=m;
        return B;
    }
    else
        return -1;
}

ll bubbleSort(int arr[], int n)
{
    ll count=0;
    int i, j;
    int flag; //
    for (i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
    {
        flag = 0; //
        for (j = 0; j < n - i - 1; j++)
        {
            if (arr[j] > arr[j + 1])
            {
                flag = 1;
                count++;
                swap(arr[j], arr[j + 1]);
            }
        }


        for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
            cout<<arr[i]<<" ";
        cout<<endl;


        if (flag == 0)
            break;
        //count++;
    }
    return count;
}

ll solve()
{
    ll n,k;
    ll numNeg=0;
    cin>>n>>k;
    vll arr;
    for(ll i=0;i<n;i++)
    {
        ll tp;
        cin>>tp;
        if(tp<0)
            numNeg++;
        arr.push_back(tp);
    }

    sort(arr.begin(),arr.end());

    if(numNeg<=k)
    {
        ll sum=0;
        for(ll i=0;i<n;i++)
        {
            if(arr[i]<0)
                sum+=(-1)*arr[i];
            else
                sum+=arr[i];
        }
        return sum;
    }
    else
    {
        ll sum=0;
        for(ll i=0;i<k;i++)
            arr[i]=arr[i]*(-1);
    /*for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
        cout<<arr[i]<<" ";
    cout<<endl;*/           
        for(ll i=0;i<n;i++)
        {
            if(arr[i]>0)
                sum+=arr[i];
        }
        return sum;
    }

}

int main()
{
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(0), cin.tie(0), cout.tie(0);
    ll t;
    cin>>t;
    //t=1;
    while(t--) {
        /*if(solve())
            cout<<"YES"<<endl;
        else
            cout<<"NO"<<endl;*/
        //solve();
        cout<<solve()<<endl;
    }

    return 0;
}

9/18/21, 11:01 AM - +91 91371 77349: import java.util.*;
class Solution {
    public static void main(String args[]) {
		
		// Write code here
        Scanner s =new Scanner(System.in);
        int n=s.nextInt();
        int[] arr=new int[n];
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++){
            arr[i]=s.nextInt();
        }
        int x=s.nextInt();
        int result=-1;
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++){
            if(arr[i]==x){
                System.out.println(i);
                break;
            }
        }
        System.out.print(result);
	}
}

9/18/21, 5:30 PM - +91 91371 77349: Check out the "sort012 Problem" from Coding Ninjas 
https://www.codingninjas.com/codestudio/guided-paths/basics-of-c/content/118787/offering/1381280
9/18/21, 5:30 PM - +91 91371 77349: import java.util.Scanner;

class Solution {

	static void sort012(int arr[], int n) {
		//Write your code here
        int low=0;
        int mid=0;
        int high=n-1;
        while(mid<high){
            if(arr[mid]==0){
                int temp=arr[low];
                arr[low]=arr[mid];
                arr[mid]=temp;
                low++;
                mid++;
            }
            else if(arr[mid]==1){
                mid++;
            }
            else{
                int temp=arr[high];
                arr[high]=arr[mid];
                arr[mid]=temp;
                high--;
            }
        }
	}

	public static void main(String args[]) {
		Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
		int t = sc.nextInt();

		while (t > 0) {
			int n = sc.nextInt();
			int arr[] = new int[n];
			for (int i = 0 ; i < n ; i++) {
				arr[i] = sc.nextInt();
			}
			sort012(arr, n);
			for (int i = 0 ; i < n; i++) {
				System.out.print(arr[i] + " ");
			}
			System.out.println();;
			t--;
		}
	}
}

a=input().split(" ")
b=input().split(" ")
c=input().split(" ")
list=[]
for i in a:

        list.append(i)

    if i not in b:
        list.append(i)

10/1/21, 1:15 PM - +91 86175 99591: this https://practice.geeksforgeeks.org/problems/find-second-largest-element2406/1 is the problem right?
```public static int print2largest(int a[],int n) 
    {
        //CODE HERE
        if(n==1) return -1;
        int m[]={Math.max(a[0],a[1]),-1};
        if(a[0]!=a[1]) m[1]=a[0]+a[1]-m[0];
        for(int i=2;i<n;i++)
        if(a[i]>m[0])
        {
            m[1]=m[0];
            m[0]=a[i];
        }
        else if(a[i]<m[0] && a[i]>m[1]) m[1]=a[i];
        return m[1];
    }```
here is a simple soln. why overcomplicate it with heaps and shit?

10/12/21, 9:03 PM - +91 62050 65572: #include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

int main()
{
    int T;
    cin>>T;
    while(T--)
    {
       int n,x,y,ans=0;
       
       cin>>n>>x>>y;
       
       vector<int> vec{ 2, 3, 5, 7 };
       
       int same = n;
       
       while(n > 1)
       {
           int i; 
           for(i=0;i<vec.size();i++)
           {
               if(n % vec[i] == 0)
               {
                   if(vec[i] % 2 == 0)
                   ans += x;
                   else 
                   ans += y;
                   
                   n = n/vec[i];
                   break;
               }
           }
           
           if(i == vec.size())
           break;
       }
       
       int t1;
       
       if(same % 2 == 0)
       t1 = x;
       else
       t1 = y;
       
       cout<<max(ans,t1)<<endl;
       
    }
}

10/29/21, 8:42 PM - Anand: public int tribonacci(int n) {
    if(n == 2) return 1;
    if(n == 0 || n == 1 || n < 0 ) return n;

    return tribonacci(n-1)+tribonacci(n-2)+tribonacci(n-3);
}

11/10/21, 10:28 AM - +91 89201 24609: DecenTile Games have launched a new First Person Shooter soldier game, called the Call of War, that young gamers can play on their website.
Our protagonist, Ninja (played by you) is a soldier whose mission is to kill all the enemies plotting against you. Your enemies are standing in a circle, numbered clockwise from 1 to n. Initially, the i-th enemy has ai health.
You have only one pistol, and infinite bullet magazines. You have to shoot the enemies in order to kill them. Each bullet fired at the enemy decreases their health by 1 (deals 1 damage to it). When the health of some enemy i becomes 0 or less than 0, he dies and his grenade drops down and explodes, dealing bi damage to the next enemy (enemy i+1, if i<n, or enemy 1, if i=n). If the next enemy is already dead, then nothing happens. If the frag from the grenade kills the next enemy, even he drops a grenade, damaging the enemy after him and possibly triggering another explosion, and so on.
You have to calculate the minimum number of bullets you need in order to kill all the enemies and win the game.
Input Format:
First line of input will contain T(number of test cases), each test case follows as.
Line 1: contain an integer N
Next N line contains two space separated integers a and b
Output Format:
For each test case print the output in a new line.
Constraints:
1 <= T <= 100
1 <= N <= 10^4
1 <= a, b <= 10^12
Sample Input:
1
3
7 15
2 14
5 3
Sample Output:
6

11/10/21, 11:02 AM - +91 81269 13538: #include <bits/stdc++.h>

using namespace std;

int main()
{
    int t;
    cin>>t;
    while(t--)
    {
        int n;
        cin>>n;
        int a[n],b[n];
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
        {
            cin>>a[i]>>b[i];
        }
        vector<pair<int,int>>v;
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
        {
            v.push_back({a[i],b[i]});
        }
        sort(v.begin(),v.end());
        int cnt=v[0].first;
        for(int i=1;i<n;i++)
        {
            if(v[i-1].second<v[i].first)
            {
                cnt+=v[i].first-v[i-1].second;
            }
            
        }
        cout<<cnt;
        
    }

    return 0;
}

*The 5th National Engineering Olympiad*!

The National Engineering Olympiad is a competitive platform for the engineering students to showcase your aptitude skills and represent yourself at the national level.

Register now!
https://nationalolympiad.org/?refid=190121911

*The Rounds:*
Similar to the previous four editions of the National Engineering Olympiad, the exam will be conducted in two rounds.

Round 1: Basic Aptitude Test- 60 mins, 60 questions. 
Dates are 24th & 25th November 2021. 
Round 2: Technical Ability Test- Online MCQ Exam based on your syllabus of the year & branch you are studying in. 

*Please Note:*
Both rounds are to be attempted _online_ from your home!

*The Rewards:* 

_For the top performers:_
-Top 10 of each year & branch will be awarded a Medal of Excellence.
-Top 30 of each year & branch will be awarded a Certificate of Excellence.

_For all participants:_
-Guaranteed Internships in various domains.
-A national-level participation certificate.
-Career-oriented webinars by experts for free.

(More details are available on the website)

Hurry up! Registrations have started!
Visit https://nationalolympiad.org/?refid=190121911

11/27/21, 3:33 PM - +91 98182 46421: Writing the algorithm here in pseudo code,

find first zero index

for curr_index, num in array:
    if num is not zero and first_zero_index < curr_index:
          swap(curr_idx, first_zero_index)
           first_zero_index = curr_idx
           curr_idx -= 1

12/21/21, 6:57 PM - +91 91371 77349: package Recursion;

import java.util.Scanner;

public class NumberOfDigits {
	public static int digits(int n) {
		if(n%10==1) {
			return 1;
		}
		int smallAns=digits(n/10);
		int ans=smallAns+1;
		return ans;
	}

	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		Scanner s =new Scanner(System.in);
		int n=s.nextInt();
		System.out.println(digits(n));

	}
}

1/5/22, 8:53 AM - +91 91371 77349: package Recursion;

public class FirstIndexOfANumber {
	public static int FirstIndex(int [] arr,int number,int index) {
		if(index==arr.length-1 && arr[index]!=number) {
			return -1;
		}
		if(arr[index]==number) {
			return index;
		}
		return FirstIndex(arr,number,index+1);	 
	}
	public static int FirstIndex(int [] arr,int number) {
		return FirstIndex(arr,number,0);
	}
	
	public static void main(String[] args) {
		// TODO Auto-generated method stub
		int [] arr= {12,45,66,13,23,67,13};
		System.out.println(FirstIndex(arr,13));
	}

}

1/5/22, 8:55 AM - +91 91371 77349: First Index of Number

Given an array of length N and an integer x, you need to find and return the first index of integer x present in the array. Return -1 if it is not present in the array.
First index means, the index of first occurrence of x in the input array.
Do this recursively. Indexing in the array starts from 0.
Input Format :
Line 1 : An Integer N i.e. size of array
Line 2 : N integers which are elements of the array, separated by spaces
Line 3 : Integer x
Output Format :
first index or -1
Constraints :
1 <= N <= 10^3
Sample Input :
4
9 8 10 8
8
Sample Output :
1

1/8/22, 7:23 PM - +91 88253 53912: Question -->
Sorcerer Sequence
Sorcerer Sequence is a series of integer numbers in which the first term starts with a positive integer and the remaining terms are generated from the immediate previous term using the below recurrence relation :

a
k
+
1
 = 
a
1
/
2
k
 for even  
a
k

a
k
+
1
 = 
a
3
/
2
k
 for odd  
a
k

Given a number 
N
 your task is to print the space-separated Sorcerer Sequence using this number as the first term of the sequence.

Input Format
First-line contains an integer 
T
, where 
T
 is the number of test cases.
For every Test case, there is only one integer input 
N
.

Output Format
For each test case, in a new line print, the space-separated Sorcerer sequence for number 
N
 as the first term of the sequence.

Constraints
1
<=
T
<=
100

1
<=
N
<=
100

Time Limit
1 second

Example
Input1
2
8
5

Output
8 2 1
5 11 36 6 2 1

Explanation
In the first test case, we start with 
8
 and use the above formula to get the next terms, until we reach 
1
.
In the second test case, we start with 
5
, next digit is (sqrt(5)*sqrt(5)*sqrt(5)) = 
11
, and so on.



Solution -->

import java.util.*;
import java.io.*;
import java.lang.*;

public class Main {
  public static void main(String args[]) throws IOException {
    
    //write your code here
    Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
    int t,n;
    t = sc.nextInt();
    while(t-->0){
      n = sc.nextInt();
      System.out.print(n);
      while(n>1){
        if(n%2==0) n = (int)Math.pow(n,0.5);
        else n = (int)Math.pow(n,1.5);
        System.out.print(" " + n);
        
      }
      System.out.println();
    }
  }
}

1/20/22, 9:29 PM - +91 70115 09528: public static void generateP(List<Integer> arr, int i) {
        if (i == arr.size() - 1) {
            global.add(arr);
            return;
        }
        for (int j = i; j < arr.size(); j++) {
            swapInt(arr, i, j);
            generateP(arr, i + 1);
            swapInt(arr, i, j);
        }
    }

    public static void swapInt(List<Integer> list, int i, int j) {
        int temp = list.get(i);
        list.set(i, list.get(j));
        list.set(j, temp);
    }

//Initial Template for Java

import java.io.*;
import java.util.*;

class Array {
	
	public static void main (String[] args) {
		Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
		
		//Input testcases
		int testcases = sc.nextInt();
		while(testcases-- > 0)
		{
		    //input size of Array
		    int sizeOfArray = sc.nextInt();
		    int arr[] = new int[sizeOfArray];
		    
		    //input elements in the Array
		    for(int i = 0; i < sizeOfArray-1; i++)
		    {
		        int x;
		        x = sc.nextInt();
		        arr[i] = x;
		    }
		    
		    //input index
		    int index = sc.nextInt();
		    //input element
		    int element = sc.nextInt();
		    
		    Solution obj = new Solution();
		    
		    //calling insertAtIndex() function
		    obj.insertAtIndex(arr,sizeOfArray,index,element);
		    
		    //Printing the array for verification
		    for(int i = 0; i < sizeOfArray;i++)
		    {
		        System.out.print(arr[i] + " ");
		        
		    }
		    
		    System.out.println();
		}
	}
}


// } Driver Code Ends


//User function Template for Java

//Complete this function, Geeks
class Solution
{
    // You need to insert the given element at the given index. 
    // After inserting the elements at index, elements
    // from index onward should be shifted one position ahead
    // You may assume that the array already has sizeOfArray - 1
    // elements.
    public void insertAtIndex(int arr[],int sizeOfArray,int index,int element)
    {
        //Your code here, Geeks
        int [] arr1=new int [sizeOfArray+1];
        
        
        for(int old=0,naya=0;naya<arr1.length;naya++){
            if(naya==index){
                arr1[naya]=element;
            }
            else{
                arr1[naya]=arr[old];
                old++;
            }
        }

4/9/22, 10:10 PM - +91 90395 87447: Hi all
4/9/22, 10:10 PM - +91 90395 87447: bool isValid(int i, int j, int n, int m, vector<vector<int>>& grid){
        if(i>=0 && i<n && j>=0 && j<m && grid[i][j] == 1){
            return true;
        }
        return false;
    }
    
    int orangesRotting(vector<vector<int>>& grid) {
        int n = grid.size();
        int m = grid[0].size();
        
        int fresh = 0, time = 0;
        queue<pair<int, int>>q;
        
        for(int i=0; i<n; i++){
            for(int j=0; j<m; j++){
                if(grid[i][j] == 2){
                    // push into queue
                    q.push({i, j});
                }
                else if(grid[i][j] == 1){
                    fresh++;
                }
            }
        }
        if(fresh == 0) return 0;
        
        // start BFS traversal
        while(!q.empty()){
            
            int size_q = q.size();
            int temp = 0;
            while(size_q != 0){
                
                pair<int, int>p = q.front();
                q.pop();
                
                int x1 = p.first;
                int y1 = p.second;
                
                int ax[4] = { 1, -1, 0, 0};
                int ay[4] = { 0, 0 , 1 , -1};
                
                for(int i=0; i<4; i++){
                    int x = ax[i] + x1;
                    int y = ay[i] + y1;
                    
                    if(isValid(x, y, n, m, grid)){
                        temp++;
                        grid[x][y] = 2;
                        q.push({x, y});
                    }
                }
                
                size_q--;
                
            }
            if(temp != 0) time++;
        }
        
        
        
        // we checked if any fresh oranges are still there
        for(int i=0; i<n; i++){
            for(int j=0; j<m; j++){
                if(grid[i][j] == 1){
                    time = 0;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        
        
        
        return (time == 0) ? -1 : time ;
        
    }

1/20/23, 4:36 PM - +91 70115 09528: can anyone help me in a tree question

https://leetcode.com/problems/vertical-order-traversal-of-a-binary-tree/description/
1/20/23, 4:37 PM - +91 91238 47093: Hold
1/20/23, 4:37 PM - +91 91238 47093: Use hashmap to solve this question
1/20/23, 4:37 PM - +91 91238 47093: With multiset
1/20/23, 4:37 PM - +91 70115 09528: public class TreeNode {
    public int val;
    public TreeNode left;
    public TreeNode right;

    public TreeNode() {
    }

    public TreeNode(int val) {
        this.val = val;
    }

    public TreeNode(int val, TreeNode left, TreeNode right) {
        this.val = val;
        this.left = left;
        this.right = right;
    }
}


public class Leetcode987 {
    public static void main(String[] args) {

    }

    public static List<List<Integer>> verticalTraversal(TreeNode root) {
        List<List<Integer>> res = new ArrayList<>();
        if (root == null) {
            return res;
        }
        HashMap<Integer, LevelValueList> map = new HashMap<>();
        Queue<DistanceNodePair> queue = new ArrayDeque<>();
        queue.offer(new DistanceNodePair(root, 0));
        int minVerticalLevel = 0;
        int maxVerticalLevel = 0;
        int horizontalLevel = 0;
        while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
            int currLevelCount = queue.size();
            for (int i = 0; !queue.isEmpty() && i < currLevelCount; i++) {
                DistanceNodePair front = queue.poll();
                minVerticalLevel = Math.min(front.horizontalDistance, minVerticalLevel);
                maxVerticalLevel = Math.max(front.horizontalDistance, maxVerticalLevel);
                if (map.containsKey(front.horizontalDistance)) {
                    LevelValueList levelValueList = map.get(front.horizontalDistance);
                    if (levelValueList.level == horizontalLevel && levelValueList.list.get(levelValueList.list.size() - 1) > front.node.val) {
                        int temp = levelValueList.list.get(levelValueList.list.size() - 1);
                        levelValueList.list.set(levelValueList.list.size() - 1, front.node.val);
                        levelValueList.list.add(temp);
                    } else {
                        levelValueList.list.add(front.node.val);
                    }
                    levelValueList.level = horizontalLevel;
                } else {
                    List<Integer> nestedList = new ArrayList<>();
                    nestedList.add(front.node.val);
                    LevelValueList levelValueList = new LevelValueList(horizontalLevel, nestedList);
                    map.put(front.horizontalDistance, levelValueList);
                }
                if (front.node.left != null) {
                    queue.offer(new DistanceNodePair(front.node.left, front.horizontalDistance - 1));
                }
                if (front.node.right != null) {
                    queue.offer(new DistanceNodePair(front.node.right, front.horizontalDistance + 1));
                }
            }
            horizontalLevel++;
        }
        for (int i = minVerticalLevel; i <= maxVerticalLevel; i++) {
            res.add(map.get(i).list);
        }
        return res;
    }
}

class LevelValueList {
    int level;
    List<Integer> list;

    public LevelValueList(int level, List<Integer> list) {
        this.level = level;
        this.list = list;
    }
}

class DistanceNodePair {
    TreeNode node;
    int horizontalDistance;

    public DistanceNodePair(TreeNode node, int horizontalDistance) {
        this.node = node;
        this.horizontalDistance = horizontalDistance;
    }
}
1/20/23, 4:37 PM - +91 70115 09528: what i have done wrong
1/20/23, 4:38 PM - +91 91238 47093: class Solution {
public:
    void dfs(TreeNode* root,int row ,int col, map<int, map<int, multiset<int>>>& mp)
    {
        if(!root)
            return;
        mp[col][row].insert(root->val);
        dfs(root->left,row+1,col-1,mp);
        dfs(root->right,row+1,col+1,mp);
    }
    
    vector<vector<int>> verticalTraversal(TreeNode* root) {
        vector<vector<int>> ans;
        map<int, map<int, multiset<int>>> mp;
        dfs(root,0,0,mp);
        for(auto x: mp)
        {
            vector<int> temp;
            for(auto y: x.second)
                for(auto z: y.second)
                    temp.push_back(z);
            ans.push_back(temp);
        }
        return ans;
    }
};
